meta.certificationName: Microsoft Certified: Fabric Data Engineer Associate (DP-700)
meta.title: MS-FDE-2: OneLake, Pipelines, Real-Time and Governance

questions.stem: A Fabric team wants a single logical data lake that can be shared by lakehouses, warehouses, and real-time analytics items without copying files. Which Fabric component provides that foundation?
questions.explanation: OneLake is Fabric's unified storage layer, so multiple Fabric experiences can work from the same underlying data without separate lake copies. The other choices are orchestration or governance features, not the storage foundation.

questions.stem: A data engineer needs to expose files from an external ADLS Gen2 account inside Fabric without physically duplicating the data. What should the engineer create?
questions.explanation: A shortcut lets OneLake reference external storage locations while avoiding a copy. Warehouses and semantic models do not surface arbitrary external files directly, and mirrored databases target operational sources rather than generic lake files.

questions.stem: In a medallion architecture implemented in Fabric lakehouses, which layer should contain data that has already been conformed, validated, and modeled for business use?
questions.explanation: Gold data is curated for business consumption. Bronze holds raw landed data and Silver is typically cleaned and standardized but not the final business-facing layer.
questions.reference: [Implement Medallion Lakehouse Architecture in Fabric - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-medallion-lakehouse-architecture)

questions.stem: A team wants Spark tables and SQL analytics to use the same Delta tables in Fabric. Which item is the best fit?
questions.explanation: A Fabric lakehouse stores files and Delta tables that Spark and SQL endpoints can use together. Dataflows and eventstreams move data, while KQL databases target high-volume event analytics.

questions.stem: Which statement about a Fabric warehouse is accurate?

options.text: It stores tables only as notebook-scoped temporary views
It is optimized for SQL analytics over managed data in OneLake
It can ingest streaming events directly from eventstreams without any table layer

questions.explanation: Fabric warehouses are SQL-focused analytic stores built on OneLake-managed data. They do not replace OneLake, and notebook temp views are unrelated to warehouse storage.

questions.stem: A team needs to orchestrate a daily sequence that copies source data, runs a notebook, and then sends a failure notification if the notebook fails. Which Fabric feature is designed for this orchestration?
questions.explanation: Fabric pipelines orchestrate multi-step workflows and dependencies. Monitoring hub observes runs, but it does not define execution logic.

questions.stem: Which Fabric capability is best when a data engineer wants low-code ingestion and transformation with Power Query before writing to a destination?
questions.explanation: Dataflow Gen2 provides Power Query-based transformation and loading. Eventhouse and Spark job definitions serve different processing styles, and shortcuts do not transform data.
questions.reference: [Differences between Dataflow Gen1 and Dataflow Gen2 - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/dataflows-gen2-overview)

questions.stem: A pipeline activity must use an output value from an earlier activity to build a file path for a later copy step. What should the engineer use?
questions.explanation: Dynamic content expressions let pipelines bind outputs and parameters between activities. Measures, result cache, and smoothing are unrelated.

questions.stem: A Fabric team promotes the same pipeline across development, test, and production while keeping environment-specific connection settings separate. Which feature should they use?
questions.explanation: Deployment pipelines are designed for stage-based promotion, and parameterized settings help keep environment-specific values separate. The other choices do not provide controlled lifecycle promotion.

questions.stem: A notebook in a pipeline intermittently fails because the source system throttles requests. What is the best first mitigation inside the orchestration layer?

options.text: Configure retry policy for the activity
Convert the notebook to a warehouse

questions.explanation: Retry policy addresses transient failures such as throttling at the activity level. The other options do not target transient orchestration failures.

questions.stem: A Fabric notebook needs to transform a large Delta table with reusable code and distributed execution. Which compute experience is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Spark notebooks are built for distributed data engineering workloads in Fabric. Reports, scorecards, and dashboards are consumption artifacts.

questions.stem: A data engineer wants a reusable, scheduled Spark workload without opening the notebook interactively. Which Fabric artifact is best suited?
questions.explanation: Spark job definitions package notebook or code execution for scheduled or automated runs. Shortcuts and semantic models do not execute Spark transformations.

questions.stem: Why do Fabric lakehouses commonly use Delta tables instead of plain parquet folders for curated data?

options.text: Delta tables add ACID transactions and table semantics
Delta tables require less storage than compressed parquet in every case
Delta tables eliminate the need for permissions

questions.explanation: Delta tables provide transactional consistency, schema enforcement/evolution support, and table metadata. The other statements are incorrect or overstated.

questions.stem: A notebook writes a cleaned dataset and the engineer wants downstream SQL users to query it immediately from the lakehouse SQL endpoint. What should the notebook write?

options.text: A Delta table in the lakehouse
A local CSV on the driver

questions.explanation: Writing a Delta table into the lakehouse makes the data available through the lakehouse SQL endpoint. Local files and notebook variables are not shared SQL assets.

questions.stem: Where should a Fabric administrator start to review the recent status of pipeline runs, notebook runs, and other item executions across a workspace?
questions.explanation: Monitoring hub centralizes operational run visibility for supported Fabric items. The other options do not provide execution monitoring.
questions.reference: [Monitoring hub: View and track Fabric activity - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/admin/monitoring-hub)

questions.stem: A capacity admin needs to understand whether a workspace is driving sustained capacity pressure and throttling risk. Which official Fabric tool is designed for this?
questions.explanation: The Fabric Capacity Metrics app helps analyze utilization and capacity behavior. Git integration and security features do not provide capacity telemetry.
questions.reference: [What is the Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics app? - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/enterprise/metrics-app)

questions.stem: A scheduled dataflow completed later than expected. Which metric would most directly help explain whether the issue was related to constrained Fabric capacity rather than a logic error?
questions.explanation: Capacity utilization trends can reveal resource pressure and throttling. Viewer counts, endorsement, and theme settings are not causal performance signals.
questions.reference: [What is the Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics app? - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/enterprise/metrics-app)

questions.stem: A Fabric engineer wants to reduce repeated full scans of a large event table by physically organizing optimized analytic data for query access. Which design choice is most relevant?

options.text: Curate and partition the Delta tables appropriately
Replace all Delta tables with CSV files

questions.explanation: Physical table design such as partitioning and curated Delta storage matters for scan efficiency. The other choices do not improve engine performance.

questions.stem: A team is deciding between querying raw bronze files and curated gold tables for executive dashboards. Which choice usually provides better performance and consistency?

options.text: Gold tables because they are curated for analytics
Bronze files because raw data is always smaller
Either is identical because OneLake removes all physical differences
Eventstreams because dashboards are always streaming

questions.explanation: Gold tables are modeled for business use and usually avoid repeated cleansing logic at query time. Bronze is raw landing data and typically less optimized for direct BI use.
questions.reference: [Implement Medallion Lakehouse Architecture in Fabric - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-medallion-lakehouse-architecture)

questions.stem: Which Fabric component is designed to ingest, transform, and route streaming events from sources such as Event Hubs in near real time?
questions.explanation: Eventstream is Fabric's streaming ingestion and routing experience. Warehouses and deployment pipelines are not event routers.

questions.stem: A team wants to query high-volume telemetry with KQL and retain the data in a store built for time-series and log-style analytics. Which Fabric item should they use?
questions.explanation: Eventhouse is built for KQL-based analytics over large event datasets. Dataflows and apps are not KQL event stores.

questions.stem: Which language is most closely associated with querying data in a Fabric Eventhouse?
questions.explanation: Eventhouse uses Kusto Query Language for interactive analytics. DAX and MDX serve semantic models, and M is Power Query.

questions.stem: A real-time engineer needs to branch one incoming event feed so that some events land in an Eventhouse and others trigger downstream processing. What Fabric capability supports this pattern?
questions.explanation: Eventstreams can transform and route incoming events to destinations. Widgets, subscriptions, and perspectives are unrelated.

questions.stem: A KQL query in Fabric should return only rows where a device produced more than 100 events in the last hour. Which KQL operation style is required?

options.text: Aggregate with `summarize`, then filter the result

questions.explanation: This requires aggregation and then filtering grouped results, a typical KQL pattern using `summarize`. The other choices do not solve the query requirement.

questions.stem: A workspace admin wants to grant a user access to a specific Fabric item without making the user a full workspace admin. What should the admin use?

options.text: Item or workspace permissions based on the Fabric permission model

questions.explanation: Fabric supports granular permission assignment through its permission model instead of requiring workspace admin for every access need. The other choices are unrelated.

questions.stem: An organization wants Fabric assets and lineage to be discoverable through Microsoft Purview. Which integration is most relevant?

options.text: Microsoft Purview integration for Fabric governance

questions.explanation: Purview integration supports governance scenarios such as discovery and lineage. The other options are analytics or notebook features.
questions.reference: [Use Microsoft Purview to Govern Microsoft Fabric - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/governance/microsoft-purview-fabric)

questions.stem: A team wants analysts to discover trusted datasets more easily and understand where tables originated. Which governance outcome is most directly supported by Fabric plus Purview capabilities?

options.text: Guaranteed elimination of all duplicate data

questions.explanation: Purview-related governance focuses on cataloging, lineage, and discoverability. It does not guarantee performance tuning or data deduplication.
questions.reference: [Use Microsoft Purview to Govern Microsoft Fabric - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/governance/microsoft-purview-fabric)

questions.stem: Which statement best describes the relationship between OneLake and Fabric experiences?

options.text: Each experience must keep its own isolated storage account
OneLake underpins multiple Fabric experiences with a unified data estate
OneLake works only for Power BI reports
OneLake is used only for notebook temp files

questions.explanation: Fabric experiences share OneLake as the common storage foundation. The other answers incorrectly narrow or misstate its role.

questions.stem: A pipeline should run only after an upstream copy activity succeeds. Which pipeline concept enables this behavior?
questions.explanation: Activity dependencies define ordering and success/failure paths between tasks in Fabric pipelines. The other choices do not orchestrate workflow order.

questions.stem: A data engineer is deciding whether to store curated structured data as files only or as managed tables in the lakehouse. Which option better supports direct SQL endpoint usage?
questions.explanation: Managed Delta tables integrate naturally with the lakehouse SQL endpoint. Raw folders alone require extra interpretation and are less convenient for governed SQL access.

questions.stem: Which Fabric capability is most useful for seeing whether a specific notebook run failed because of an execution error versus still being queued?
questions.explanation: Monitoring hub exposes run states and details helpful for distinguishing queued, running, and failed executions. The other options are not run-diagnostics tools.
questions.reference: [Monitoring hub: View and track Fabric activity - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/admin/monitoring-hub)

questions.stem: A Fabric team wants to minimize repeated transformation cost for business users who run the same cleansed query logic every day. What design is usually best?

options.text: Persist curated transformed tables for reuse
Force each dashboard to transform bronze data independently

questions.explanation: Persisting curated transformed tables shifts repeated work out of every consumer query. Recomputing from bronze repeatedly usually costs more and increases inconsistency.
questions.reference: [Implement Medallion Lakehouse Architecture in Fabric - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-medallion-lakehouse-architecture)

questions.stem: Which Fabric item is the better destination for interactive time-series analysis over streaming data using KQL semantics?
questions.explanation: Eventhouse is purpose-built for that style of analysis. Dataflows and deployment pipelines are not event analytics stores.

questions.stem: A user writes KQL to filter records before projecting selected columns. What is the main reason to filter early in the query?

options.text: To reduce the amount of data processed downstream
To create a new Fabric capacity

questions.explanation: Filtering early commonly reduces downstream processing and improves clarity. It does not change query language, remove permissions, or create capacity.

questions.stem: A data engineer must share read access to a lakehouse table with a small group while keeping broader workspace edit rights restricted. What is the best principle to follow?

options.text: Always make all consumers workspace admins

questions.explanation: Least privilege is the appropriate governance principle. Broad admin rights and moving data out of governed storage weaken control.

questions.stem: Why are shortcuts useful in large Fabric estates?

options.text: They allow logical access to data without creating another physical copy in OneLake
They automatically convert SQL to KQL
They are required for every lakehouse table

questions.explanation: Shortcuts help avoid unnecessary duplication by pointing to existing data locations. They do not replace permissions or language features.

questions.stem: A team wants business-friendly transformation steps but also needs to schedule them in a larger workflow with dependencies. Which combination best fits?

options.text: Dataflow Gen2 inside a Fabric pipeline
Semantic model inside a report theme

questions.explanation: Dataflow Gen2 handles low-code transformation, and pipelines orchestrate the end-to-end workflow. The other combinations do not provide that pattern.
questions.reference: [Differences between Dataflow Gen1 and Dataflow Gen2 - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/dataflows-gen2-overview)

questions.stem: A Fabric notebook should be reusable across environments. What practice helps most?

options.text: Parameterize paths and settings instead of hardcoding them
Embed workspace IDs in every cell output
Depend on manual cell execution order

questions.explanation: Parameterization improves portability and automation. Hardcoded environment IDs and manual execution reduce reuse.

questions.stem: A capacity appears healthy, but one pipeline still fails daily at the same transformation step. Which conclusion is best?

options.text: The issue may be logic or data-specific rather than overall capacity pressure
Capacity metrics prove the notebook code is correct

questions.explanation: If overall capacity is healthy, a repeated failure at the same step points more toward workload logic or source data issues. The other conclusions are unsupported.
questions.reference: [Monitoring hub: View and track Fabric activity - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/admin/monitoring-hub)

questions.stem: A Fabric architect needs faster BI queries and simpler governance for commonly used dimensions and facts. Which pattern is most appropriate?

options.text: Publish curated gold tables in the lakehouse or warehouse for shared consumption
Ask every analyst to keep a private copy of raw bronze files
Use eventstreams as the primary BI semantic layer for all cases
Store final metrics only in notebook output

questions.explanation: Curated shared analytic tables improve reuse, performance, and governance. Private raw copies and notebook outputs weaken consistency.
